title: 1.1: Creates Compiler.Parse(src, filename string, wrap bool) in js/compiler/compiler.go returning (*ast.Program, string, error)

annotations.description: Verifies that the compiler exposes a Parse method with signature (src string, filename string, wrap bool) returning (*ast.Program, string, error), allowing callers to access the AST before compilation.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 42-56: new Parse() method with signature (src, filename string, wrap bool) (prg *ast.Program, finalCode string, err error).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states: 'The new Parse method should return an AST that callers can then compile themselves using sobek.CompileAST().' The interface specifies: Name: Compiler.Parse, Input: src string, filename string, wrap bool, Output: (prg *ast.Program, finalCode string, err error). [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must expose a Parse method matching the specified interface.

title: 1.2: Updates the CommonJS module loader call site in js/modules/cjsmodule.go to use Parse followed by sobek.CompileAST

annotations.description: Verifies that the CommonJS module loader is updated to call Parse to get the AST, then compile it using sobek.CompileAST().
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 316-335: cjsModuleFromString calls c.Parse() then sobek.CompileAST(astProgram, true) separately.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states: 'Update all existing call sites to use the new Parse method followed by sobek.CompileAST().' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] js/modules/cjsmodule.go has cjsModuleFromString() that uses the compiler. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must update the call site in js/modules/cjsmodule.go to use Parse then sobek.CompileAST.

title: 1.3: Separates parsing logic from compilation in js/compiler/compiler.go by returning AST instead of compiled program

annotations.description: Verifies that the internal implementation returns *ast.Program from parsing without calling sobek.CompileAST internally, delegating compilation to callers.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 104-198: parseImpl() returns (*ast.Program, string, error), with line 151 returning (prg, code, nil) instead of calling sobek.CompileAST.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states: 'Refactor it to separate parsing from compilation so callers can inspect or modify the code structure before compilation.' [3: Logical Necessity] To expose intermediate representation, parsing must return AST without compiling. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must have parsing return AST without internally calling sobek.CompileAST.

title: 1.4: Corrects `sourcemappingurl` to `sourceMappingURL` in the inline source map directive in js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the sourceMappingURL directive uses proper casing (sourceMappingURL not sourcemappingurl) when replacing inline source maps.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 260: code uses '//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;base64,' with proper casing.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states: 'the sourceMappingURL directive should use proper casing.' [3: Logical Necessity] Incorrect casing may cause source map loading to fail in some environments. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must use proper casing for sourceMappingURL.

title: 1.5: Adds the directive's starting index to the relative newline offset in the slice calculation in `updateInlineSourceMap` in js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the slice offset when replacing inline source maps accounts for the index position (using index+nextnewline instead of just nextnewline).
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 260: code slice uses 'code[index+nextnewline:]' instead of 'code[nextnewline:]'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states: 'the slice offset that strips the old inline source map computes the newline position relative to the directive but doesn't add the directive's starting index when slicing the full string.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] In `updateInlineSourceMap`, `nextnewline` is computed via `strings.Index(code[index:], "\n")`, making it relative to the directive position, but the original slice `code[nextnewline:]` applies it to the full string without adding `index`. [3: Logical Necessity] Without adding the directive's starting index, the slice truncates code incorrectly. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must add the directive's starting index to the relative newline offset when slicing.

title: 2.1: Adds documentation comment for the Parse method in js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the Parse method has a documentation comment describing its purpose and return values.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 42-45: documentation comment for Parse() describes returning AST that can be compiled by Sobek.
annotations.justification: [4: Qualitative Requirement] A seasoned SWE would document new public methods for maintainability. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, a thorough implementation should include documentation for the Parse method.

title: 4.1: Removes the Transform method from js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.description: Checks that the Transform method used for Babel code transformation is not removed, as it is still needed for extended compatibility mode.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 157: Transform method still called from parseImpl via ps.compiler.Transform().
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The Transform method in js/compiler/compiler.go is used for Babel code transformation in extended compatibility mode. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing it would break extended compatibility mode. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY answer that removes the Transform method triggers this negative criterion.

title: 4.2: Removes the CommonJS module wrapping functionality from js/compiler/compiler.go

annotations.description: Checks that the CommonJS wrapping logic that wraps code in (function(module, exports){...}) is preserved.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 219-241: wrap() function preserves (function(module, exports){...}) wrapping pattern.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] CommonJS modules require function wrapping to work correctly. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing this would cause CommonJS modules to fail. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY answer that removes CommonJS wrapping functionality triggers this negative criterion.
